title: Cylinder behavioral test (mice version)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bqe3r","text":"Cylinder behavioral test for left and right forepaw use in mice","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":63}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"cq3mb","text":"Important: Rats that present an asymmetric usage of the right-left forepaws at basal examination need to be excluded from the experiment. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"bkgna","text":"All behavioral tests have to be performed during the light cycle by an investigator blinded to the experimental groups.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: First allow mice to habituate to the experimental room for at least 1 h before each test
Put animals individually in a glass cylinder and the total number of left and right forepaw touches performed within 5 min was counted
Clean behavioral equipment with 70% ethanol after each animal has been tested to avoid olfactory cues
creator: Miquel Vila
